# @platform/adapters-keycloak

Keycloak adapter: OIDC token validation, realm configuration, and auth provider implementation for security-auth.

## Package identity

```text
Name: @platform/adapters-keycloak
Version: 0.1.0
Component: adapters-keycloak
Type: library
System: enterprise-react-platform
Domain: operations
Bounded context: auth-keycloak
Owner: team-security
```

## Lifecycle

```text
Stage: active
Role: adapter
Class: active.adapter
Catalog lifecycle: production
Visibility: internal
Support level: standard
Review cadence: quarterly
```

## Ownership

```text
Owner: team-security
Change control: owner-review
Promotion eligible: true
Semver policy: internal-traceable
```

## Responsibilities

- Implement the security-auth provider interface backed by Keycloak OIDC
- Validate JWT access tokens against Keycloak realm public keys
- Extract and type identity claims from Keycloak token payloads
- Manage JWKS key rotation and caching

## Non-responsibilities

- Does not define the auth provider interface (owned by security-auth)
- Does not manage Keycloak realm configuration or client registration
- Does not own session storage or session lifecycle

## Public exports and usage

```text
Public exports only: true
Deep imports allowed: false
```

- Registered at application startup as the concrete auth provider implementation
- Only imported by application entrypoints ? never by feature or domain packages

```text
application
```

```text
contract
```

### Depends on

```text

@platform/config-runtime
@platform/authorisation-runtime
```

### Provides APIs

```text
auth-keycloak-provider
```

### Consumes APIs

```text
auth-gateway
config-service
Keycloak OIDC endpoints
```

## Operational notes

- JWKS cache TTL must be shorter than Keycloak key rotation interval
- Realm and client IDs are resolved via config-runtime ? never hardcoded
